qa_pairs.question: How can one request the total memory of a node in a submission script?
qa_pairs.answer: You can configure your submission script by using the `--mem=0` option to request the total memory available on the node.

qa_pairs.question: Under what conditions is the `--mem=0` option valid for requesting total node memory?
qa_pairs.answer: The `--mem=0` option is valid only if all cores on the node are also allocated and used. In this specific case, it is possible to request the entirety of the memory associated with the node.

qa_pairs.question: What is the approximate memory per core on Béluga and Narval clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: On Béluga and Narval clusters, you typically have approximately 4 GB of memory per core, in equivalent-core terms.

qa_pairs.question: What is the advantage of requesting less memory than the default equivalent-core amount?
qa_pairs.answer: Requesting less memory than the default equivalent-core amount can help you save time by potentially reducing wait times for your task.

qa_pairs.question: Is it acceptable to request more memory than the default equivalent-core amount if a task requires it?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is acceptable to request more memory if your task genuinely requires it.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended memory buffer to prevent 'Out of Memory' (OOM) errors?
qa_pairs.answer: It is acceptable to request up to 20% more memory than what you expect to use, to ensure you don't run out.
